verdict: BUY for Income — 3.8% yield growing 6-7%, renewables growth component differentiates from peers; excellent Income portfolio utility pick

name: Spire Inc.
thesis: Spire Inc. is a pure-play natural gas distribution utility serving 1.7 million customers in Missouri, Alabama, and Mississippi; the company has a 20-year streak of consecutive annual dividend increases and a predictable earnings profile from rate-regulated gas distribution. FY2025 (Sep year-end) revenue of $2.48B with EPS of $4.37 and a 3.8% dividend yield.
revenue_trend: Declining -4.5% — FY2024 $2,593M → FY2025 $2,476M (gas cost passthrough lower)
guidance: Maintained — SR guided FY2026 EPS of $4.60-4.80; rate cases in Missouri and Alabama expected to add $40M in incremental revenue; dividend growth maintained at 3-4% annually
key_risks: Natural gas distribution faces long-term secular decline as building electrification (heat pumps) reduces gas usage
Missouri regulatory risk — rate cases subject to political pressure in a moderately challenging regulatory environment
Negative FCF is structural — gas utilities require constant capex for infrastructure replacement
earnings_call_tone: Neutral — 'Rate case outcomes have been favorable; our gas system reliability programs are reducing outages and supporting customer satisfaction; we remain committed to our 20+ year dividend growth streak'
verdict: HOLD for Income — Steady utility with consistent dividend growth but limited upside; appropriate Income portfolio diversifier but prefer NJR over SR for growth

name: NextEra Energy Inc.
thesis: NextEra Energy is the world's largest generator of wind and solar energy through its NextEra Energy Resources subsidiary, while also operating the largest regulated electric utility in Florida (FPL); the company has grown its dividend 10% annually for 10 consecutive years and targets continued 10% dividend growth through 2027. FY2025 revenue grew 11% to $27.4B with EPS of $3.30.
guidance: Raised — NEE raised FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance to $3.45-3.70 and maintained 10% annual dividend growth target through 2027; announced 10GW+ of new renewable energy contracts with AI data center operators
key_risks: Interest rate sensitivity — NEE's high P/E multiple and capital-intensive business are sensitive to rate movements
Florida hurricane risk — FPL's service territory is highly exposed to Atlantic hurricane damage to grid infrastructure
Renewable development execution at scale — managing 50GW+ of new projects requires flawless construction execution
earnings_call_tone: Bullish — CEO John Ketchum: 'The AI data center buildout is the most powerful demand signal we have ever seen; we signed 10GW of new renewable contracts with hyperscalers in 2025 alone'
verdict: BUY for Core — Largest renewable energy company, 10%/year dividend growth, AI data center tailwind; premier Core/Income utility for any portfolio

name: Eversource Energy
thesis: Eversource Energy is the largest electric utility in New England, serving Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire; the company recovered from a difficult period of offshore wind project losses ($1.5B+ write-downs in 2023) and sold its offshore wind interests for $1.1B. FY2025 revenue grew 14% to $13.55B with EPS of $4.56, and the company is refocusing on its core regulated utility franchise.
guidance: Raised — ES raised FY2026 EPS guidance to $4.80-5.00; clean energy investment plan of $23B over 5 years focused on T&D infrastructure and EV charging; dividend growth of 5-7% targeted
key_risks: New England regulatory lag — rate cases in CT and MA are subject to 12-18 month regulatory reviews limiting cost recovery timeliness
Offshore wind legacy exposure — sold interests but counterparty credit exposure remains from past deal structures
High negative FCF — substantial infrastructure investment program requires debt financing in elevated rate environment
earnings_call_tone: Neutral — 'We have turned a difficult page on offshore wind; our regulated utilities are performing well and we are executing on the largest capital program in New England utility history'
verdict: HOLD for Income — 4.1% yield with growth, offshore wind issues resolved; quality New England utility but high leverage limits upside; add selectively on weakness

name: PG&E Corporation
thesis: PG&E Corporation has completed its post-bankruptcy restructuring and is executing a $57B, 5-year capital plan to harden its California electric and gas system against wildfire risk while also meeting surging demand from data centers and EV charging; FY2025 revenue grew 2% to $24.9B with EPS of $1.18, and the recently reinstated dividend ($0.125/share) signals growing financial confidence from management.
guidance: Raised — PCG raised FY2026 EPS guidance to $1.30-1.45; initiated 10% annual dividend growth guidance; $57B capex plan funded by rate base growth providing 9-10% annual EPS growth through 2028
key_risks: Wildfire liability is existential — another major California wildfire attributable to PCG infrastructure could re-trigger bankruptcy
Regulatory approval risk in California — CPUC rate case outcomes are unpredictable in consumer-focused California regulatory environment
Ongoing criminal probation limits certain business activities and creates governance overhang
earnings_call_tone: Bullish — CEO Patti Poppe: 'Our undergrounding program is the most aggressive wildfire mitigation effort in utility history; we are confident in our path to a 9% annual EPS CAGR through 2028'
verdict: HOLD — Compelling growth narrative post-bankruptcy but wildfire liability is unacceptably concentrated risk; appropriate for growth-oriented Core with risk awareness

name: Northwest Natural Holding Company
thesis: Northwest Natural is a regulated natural gas distribution utility serving Oregon and Washington with a 65+ consecutive year dividend growth streak; FY2025 revenue grew 12% to $1.29B with EPS of $2.77, and the company has expanded into water utility and renewable natural gas to diversify beyond traditional gas distribution. Current dividend yield is 4.5% with minimal growth.
guidance: Maintained — NWN guided FY2026 EPS of $2.80-3.00; Pacific Northwest water utility acquisitions adding regulated assets; RNG (renewable natural gas) investments from food processors and farms
key_risks: Pacific Northwest regulatory environment is ESG-focused with strong push toward building electrification reducing natural gas demand
Oregon and Washington climate goals require gas utilities to demonstrate decarbonization pathways or face rate challenges
Negative FCF structurally — capex requirements exceed earnings in gas infrastructure replacement
earnings_call_tone: Neutral — 'Our water utility expansion is progressing; renewable natural gas projects are diversifying our portfolio; we remain committed to our 65-year record of consecutive dividend growth'
verdict: HOLD for Income — 65-year dividend growth streak is impressive but dividend growth rate is near zero (0.5%/yr); Pacific Northwest ESG headwinds are structural; hold for yield stability
